[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower supply a fee-on-transfer collateral token under preconditions where the token deducts a fee on every transferFrom, triggering the call sequence supplyCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, onBehalf) -> safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, msg.sender, address(this), assets), causing _position.collateral[collateralIndex] to be recorded as assets while Midnight only receives assets minus the fee, violating the invariant that recorded collateral must equal actual token balance held, causing scoped impact: inflated collateral value allows borrower to borrow more than their real collateral supports, creating bad debt? Proof idea: deploy a 1% fee-on-transfer ERC20 as collateral, supplyCollateral(1000e18), assert position.collateral == 1000e18 but token.balanceOf(midnight) == 990e18, then take a sell offer to borrow up to the inflated LLTV limit and assert the market is immediately insolvent.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: Midnight.repay] Can an unprivileged borrower repay with a fee-on-transfer loan token under preconditions where the loan token charges a fee on transferFrom, triggering the call sequence repay(market, units, onBehalf, address(0), '') -> position[id][onBehalf].debt -= units; marketState[id].withdrawable += units; safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), units), causing withdrawable to increase by units while Midnight only receives units minus the fee, violating the invariant that total withdrawable must not exceed actual loan token balance, causing scoped impact: lenders can collectively withdraw more than the protocol holds, draining the contract to insolvency? Proof idea: fuzz fee percentage in [1, 500] bps, repay N units, assert withdrawable increased by N but balanceOf(midnight) increased by N*(1-fee), then have lenders withdraw until revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/IEcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged taker execute a take where the seller receives fewer loan tokens than their debt obligation under preconditions where the loan token is fee-on-transfer and the seller is the receiver, triggering the call sequence take(...) -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets) where receiver is the seller, causing the seller to receive sellerAssets*(1-fee) while their debt is increased by units at full value, violating the invariant that a seller's debt increase must correspond to actual assets received, causing scoped impact: seller is underpaid relative to their debt obligation, effectively losing value on every borrow?
